frameworks.name: curriculum-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: staged RL curriculum trains policy to acquire complex motor skill

frameworks.name: musculoskeletal simulation
frameworks.one_line: physics-based hand model with 39 muscles as control substrate

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network policy
frameworks.one_line: RNN controller learns dexterous in-hand manipulation via RL

frameworks.name: muscle synergy analysis
frameworks.one_line: low-dimensional kinematic synergies as account of motor control

core_question: Can curriculum-based RL acquire dexterous musculoskeletal control, and do the learned policies recapitulate human-like motor synergies?
key_assumptions: biological motor control can be probed by training agents on realistic musculoskeletal models
learning without explicit low-dimensional bias still permits synergy emergence
selective inactivation reveals true effective dimensionality of control
manipulations: curriculum stages during training of the RNN policy
task of rotating two Baoding balls in the palm
selective inactivation of components of the learned control signal
key_findings: the policy controls a 39-muscle hand to rotate two Baoding balls
a small number of kinematic synergies emerges without explicit low-dimensional bias
selective inactivation reveals more contributing dimensions than synergy analysis suggests
interpretations: learned synergies are consistent with human data yet underestimate task-relevant dimensionality
musculoskeletal simulation plus RL is a viable tool for studying biological motor control
